title: VN — NAICS 221118 Other Electric Power Generation (BESS Reference)

sections.title: L7 — Ecosystem / CFJ
sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| L7 name | Grid-Connected Electrical Energy Storage Ecosystem |
| CFJ | Deliver dispatchable multi-MWh electrical energy on demand to the power system, earning revenue from flexibility, capacity, and ancillary-service markets by absorbing grid/renewable surplus and releasing it when needed |
| Framework | Moore 1993 — economic community of asset owners, EPC integrators, equipment OEMs, DSOs/TSOs, regulators, aggregators, O&M providers, financiers, recyclers coordinated around grid-storage outcomes |

---

sections.title: L6 — Process Steps (Project Lifecycle)
sections.content:
### Core L6 (sequential)

| ID | Name | Job family (verb + object) | Output types served |
|---|---|---|---|
| L6a | Site Development & Permitting | Secure buildable grid-connected site with Baugenehmigung + fire clearance | Standalone, PV+BESS, Wind+BESS, Netzbooster, C&I-BTM, HPC-buffer |
| L6b | Engineering & Design | Design multi-MWh storage asset to grid code + revenue model | All |
| L6c | Procurement & Supply | Procure battery containers, PCS, MV gear, EMS, civil materials | All |
| L6d | Construction & Integration | Install, wire, integrate BESS + PCS + MV + EMS into commissioned asset | All |
| L6e | Commissioning & Grid-Code Certification | Energize, test, obtain VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8 plant certificate + DSO acceptance | All |
| L6f | Operations & Dispatch | Dispatch charge/discharge against markets + customer loads over lifetime | All |
| L6g | Decommissioning & End-of-Life | Dismantle, ship cells to second-life or recycling per EU Battery Reg./CRMA | All |

### Horizontal L6 (parallel across core)

| ID | Name | Role |
|---|---|---|
| L6H-HSE (H) | HSE & Fire Safety | Thermal-runaway containment, Feuerwehr interface, IP rating, VdS compliance |
| L6H-QA (H) | Quality Assurance | FAT/SAT, performance guarantee testing, availability + throughput verification |
| L6H-CYB (H) | Cybersecurity & OT | IEC 62443 / BSI KritisV / NIS2 compliance for DSO-facing assets |
| L6H-REG (H) | Regulatory & Market Enablement | §19 StromNEV, FCR/aFRR prequalification, EU Battery Reg., tariff design |

---

sections.title: L5 — Sellable Units per L6
sections.content:
Horizontal L6 L5 positions omitted for brevity except where Mein Solar could serve.

### L6a Site Development & Permitting

| L5 | Description | Alternatives (tech/approach) |
|---|---|---|
| Site Acquisition & Lease Services | Greenfield/brownfield site origination | Lease, purchase, PV-site retrofit |
| Grid-Capacity Study & DSO Application | Feasibility + Netzverträglichkeitsprüfung | In-house developer, external engineering office, DSO consultancy |
| Environmental & Fire Permitting | BImSchG screening, VdS fire concept, Feuerwehr plan | Specialty fire-protection engineer, TÜV, Dekra |

### L6b Engineering & Design

| L5 | Description | Alternatives (L5 competitors) |
|---|---|---|
| Owner's Engineer Services | Independent technical advisor to asset owner | DNV, TÜV SÜD, TÜV NORD, Fichtner, 8.2 Ingenieurbüro |
| Detailed System Engineering | Battery sizing, PCS selection, SLD, protection study | In-house EPC, specialist engineering firms, OEM turnkey |
| Grid-Code Compliance Design | VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8 design package | FGW-accredited certifier, OEM engineering, independent cert engineer |
| Fire-Protection Engineering | Hazard analysis, suppression sizing, deflagration venting | VdS-certified engineers, Dekra, TÜV |

### L6c Procurement & Supply

| L5 | Description | Alternatives (dominant tech split) |
|---|---|---|
| BESS Container Supply | 20ft/40ft containerized LFP system | **LFP 85–90%** (CATL, EVE, BYD, Sungrow PowerTitan, Tesla Megapack, SolarEast, CATL EnerC) / **NMC 10–15% declining** |
| PCS (Power Conversion System) Supply | Bidirectional 4-quadrant inverter | **Sungrow ~21% EU lead**, SMA, Power Electronics, Ingeteam, ABB, Dynapower, KACO |
| MV Transformer & Switchgear Supply | 10/20 kV step-up + protection | Siemens, ABB, Schneider, Eaton, Ormazabal (SF6-free trend) |
| EMS / Dispatch Software Supply | Charge/discharge optimization layer | OEM-embedded (Tesla Autobidder, Sungrow iSolarCloud), standalone (Fluence Mosaic, Stem PowerTrack), aggregator (Entrix, Next Kraftwerke, Flexpower, Enspired) |
| Battery Cells (L3 upstream) | LFP prismatic cells | CATL, EVE, BYD, Gotion, REPT, SVolt — concentrated in CN |

### L6d Construction & Integration ← MEIN SOLAR VN SEGMENT

| L5 | Description | Alternatives (incumbent integrators) |
|---|---|---|
| **Turnkey BESS EPC Integration Services** | Single-source EPC — site → commissioned asset | **← MEIN SOLAR PRIMARY POSITION**. Incumbents: Fluence, Tesla turnkey, Sungrow turnkey, Wärtsilä, Wärtsilä Storage, Nidec ASI, Hitachi Energy, ENGIE, BayWa r.e., Enerparc, Goldbeck Solar, GreenTech Energy |
| Civil Works & Site Preparation | Foundations, trenching, fencing, Brandschutz walls | Regional Tiefbau contractors, Strabag, Bauer, specialty civil |
| Mechanical/Electrical Installation | Container placement, MV/LV cabling, SCADA wiring | Electro-contractors (Elektro-Fachbetrieb), specialty HV installers |
| Split-Contract Integration Management | Multi-vendor coordination without wrapped EPC | Owner-self, technical consultancies (DNV, Fichtner) |

### L6e Commissioning & Grid-Code Certification

| L5 | Description | Alternatives |
|---|---|---|
| FGW TR8 Plant Certification Services | Type B plant certificate dossier + DSO acceptance | TÜV NORD, TÜV SÜD, Dekra, 8.2 Ingenieurbüro, FGW-accredited engineers |
| Unit Certification (FGW TR3) | PCS + BESS unit certificate handover | PCS OEM (FGW TR3 cert delivered with inverter), independent test labs |
| SCADA / DSO Interface Commissioning | IEC 60870-5-104 / IEC 61850 signal handover | DSO engineering, OEM commissioning teams, specialty OT integrators |

### L6f Operations & Dispatch

| L5 | Description | Alternatives |
|---|---|---|
| Merchant Trading / Market Optimization | Intraday, FCR, aFRR, day-ahead optimization | **Entrix (~2.8 GW / 8.1 GWh EU under contract)**, Next Kraftwerke, Flexpower, Enspired, Tiko, Baywa Trading, Statkraft |
| Asset Management Services | Revenue tracking, reporting, compliance | In-house IPP, third-party AM (Encavis, Clean Energy Capital) |
| O&M (BESS Operations & Maintenance) | Preventive/corrective maintenance, SoC mgmt, BMS tuning | OEM O&M (Sungrow, Tesla, CATL), independent (SEVERN, GreenTech, Mein Solar O&M) |
| Cloud Asset Performance Platform | SCADA aggregation, alarms, SLA evidence | DERMS (Trilliant, GE Digital), APM (AspenTech, OSIsoft PI) |

### L6g Decommissioning & End-of-Life

| L5 | Description | Alternatives |
|---|---|---|
| Second-Life Repurposing Services | Grade-B cells → lower-C-rate applications | Voltfang, Beeplanet Factory, Connected Energy |
| Battery Recycling Services | Hydro/pyro metallurgical recovery (EU Battery Reg.) | Umicore, BASF/Redux, Northvolt Revolt, Librec, Li-Cycle |
| Dismantling & Logistics | ADR-compliant cell shipment, container recovery | Specialty hazmat logistics, cell-maker take-back |

---

sections.title: L4 — Subsystems (≥3 per L5, highlighted for Mein Solar segment)
sections.content:
### L5: Turnkey BESS EPC Integration Services ← MEIN SOLAR

| L4 Subsystem | Role |
|---|---|
| Project Management Office (PMO) | Schedule, risk, interface management |
| Design Authority / Engineering Lead | SLD, protection study, integration design |
| Procurement & Logistics Desk | Equipment sourcing, FAT coordination, shipping |
| Civil Site Team | Foundations, trenching, fencing |
| MV/LV Installation Crew | Cable pulling, switchgear, terminations |
| Commissioning & Test Team | SAT, protection relay setting, trip tests |
| Grid-Code Certification Dossier | FGW TR3/TR8 evidence + DSO acceptance package |
| Warranty & Performance Guarantee Wrapper | Single-source kWh throughput + availability SLA |

### L5: BESS Container Supply (representative)

| L4 | Role |
|---|---|
| LFP Battery Module | Prismatic cells in mechanical module |
| Battery Management System (BMS/BAMS/BCMS) | Cell balance, SoC, SoH, protection |
| Thermal Management (Liquid Cooling Loop) | Temperature uniformity ±2 °C |
| HVAC Unit | Internal setpoint maintenance |
| Fire Detection & NOVEC Suppression | Aerosol/clean-agent + deflagration venting |
| DC Bus & Fuses | Module-to-string DC connection |
| Container Envelope (20/40ft ISO HC, IP54) | Mechanical/environmental protection |

### L5: PCS Supply

| L4 | Role |
|---|---|
| IGBT/SiC Power Stage | 4-quadrant conversion |
| DC/DC Stage (optional) | Battery-to-DC-bus conditioning |
| Inverter Controller | Q(U), cos-phi, grid-forming logic |
| Line Filter | Harmonic suppression |
| Cooling System | Fan or liquid-loop cooling |
| Transformer Cabinet Interface | AC output to MV transformer |

### L5: MV Transformer & Switchgear Supply

| L4 | Role |
|---|---|
| Step-Up Transformer (0.4/10–20 kV) | Voltage transformation |
| Ring-Main Unit (SF6-free) | MV sectioning |
| Protection Relays (IEC 61850) | Overcurrent, NA-protection |
| Metering Cabinet | Billing + compliance metering |
| DSO Interface Cabinet | Remote trip, SCADA uplink |

### L5: EMS Software Supply

| L4 | Role |
|---|---|
| Forecasting Module | Price, load, solar/wind predictions |
| Dispatch Optimizer | MILP/heuristic scheduling |
| SoC/Degradation Controller | Cycle-aware set-pointing |
| Market Adapter | EPEX, FCR, aFRR API |
| SCADA Integration Layer | IEC 60870/61850 to asset |

*(L4s for remaining L5 units follow same pattern: ≥3 subsystems each — see Structured Data.)*

---

sections.title: Valid Parent-Child Transitions Check
sections.content:
| Transition | Used? | Valid? |
|---|---|---|
| L7 → L6 | 11 edges (7 core + 4 horizontal) | ✅ |
| L6 → L5 | ~28 edges | ✅ |
| L5 → L4 | ~90 edges | ✅ |
| Skipped L6a-L6c Sub-SoS | No — project lifecycle is flat, no intermediate sub-grouping needed | ✅ |

Total unit count: 1 L7 + 11 L6 + ~28 L5 + ~90 L4 = **~130 units** (above code target 20–40; acceptable for a flagship VN built to full L4 depth per HARD RULE).

---

sections.title: Phase 3 — Strategic Adjacency (Steps 04e–04g)
sections.content:
### 04e — Direct Competence Transfer Scan (LOW/MEDIUM only)

Primary position: **L5 Turnkey BESS EPC Integration** under L6d Construction & Integration.

| VN Unit | Level | Competence Required | Has It? | Effort | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Detailed System Engineering (L6b) | L5 | SLD, protection study, sizing | YES | LOW | Mein Solar already does this for own EPC scope; unbundle as standalone service |
| Grid-Code Compliance Design (L6b) | L5 | VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8 authority | YES | LOW | Native competence from PV-EPC incumbency |
| Civil Works & Site Preparation (L6d) | L5 | Foundations, trenching, fire walls | YES | LOW | Already self-performed on PV-EPC |
| MV/LV Installation (L6d) | L5 | Cabling, switchgear termination | YES | LOW | Direct carry-over from PV-EPC |
| Commissioning / SCADA Interface (L6e) | L5 | Protection relay, DSO signal handover | YES | LOW | Already executed per BESS reference project |
| O&M Services (L6f) | L5 | PCS service, BMS tuning, annual cert audit | PARTIAL | MEDIUM | PV-O&M org exists; BESS O&M adds BMS + SoC discipline (6–12 mo ramp) |
| Owner's Engineer Services (L6b) | L5 | Independent technical advisor | PARTIAL | MEDIUM | Conflict-of-interest vs. own-EPC revenue; viable as separate entity |
| FGW TR8 Plant Certification (L6e) | L5 | FGW accreditation | PARTIAL | MEDIUM | Accreditation process ~12 mo; currently subcontracted to TÜV |
| Grid-Capacity Study (L6a) | L5 | DSO feasibility + Netzverträglichkeit | PARTIAL | MEDIUM | Often done inside EPC kickoff; unbundle as pre-EPC offering |
| Fire-Protection Engineering (L6b) | L5 | VdS hazard analysis, suppression sizing | PARTIAL | MEDIUM | Currently subcontracted; bring in-house with VdS-certified hire |
| *(HIGH-effort units excluded: cell/container mfg, PCS mfg, MV equipment mfg, merchant trading, recycling)* | | | | | |

### 04f — Strategic Adjacency Expansion (within L6d segment)

Current L6d siblings to Mein Solar's L5 position:

| Adjacent L5 | Incumbents | Competence Gap | Investment | Strategic Fit (1–10) | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Civil Works & Site Prep (standalone) | Regional Tiefbau, Strabag | None meaningful — already self-performed | <6 mo, <€0.5M | 6 | Low-margin commodity; only valuable as bundled EPC input, not standalone (~90% overlap) |
| MV/LV Installation (standalone) | Regional Elektro-Fachbetriebe, specialty HV | None meaningful — already self-performed | <6 mo, <€0.5M | 5 | Same logic; standalone sale cannibalizes EPC margin (~85% overlap) |
| Split-Contract Integration Management | DNV, Fichtner, 8.2 Ingenieurbüro | Owner's-engineer independence posture | 6–12 mo, €0.5–1M | 4 | Direct conflict of interest with turnkey-EPC business; unlikely viable (~40% overlap) |

**Verdict:** horizontal expansion within L6d is low-value — Mein Solar already occupies the most valuable L5. Real adjacency lies **cross-segment** (covered in 04e and FP-extension markets).

### 04g — Vertical Move-Up Assessment

| Current | Target (one up) | Must Add | Competitors at Target | Feasibility | Economic Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| L5 Turnkey EPC under L6d | **L6d Construction & Integration (own the segment)** | Already owns most of L6d via single-source EPC — arguably already there | Fluence, Tesla turnkey, Sungrow turnkey, Wärtsilä | **HIGH** | Mein Solar is definitionally the L6d integrator for its own projects; "moving up" means becoming the **L6d integrator for others' projects** (tier-1 EPC-for-EPC). TAM at L6d ≈ €5–8B DE BESS EPC market 2026–2030. Plausible organic path via scale-up, references, balance sheet |
| L6d Construction & Integration | **L7 Grid-Connected Electrical Energy Storage Ecosystem** (full asset owner / IPP) | Project finance, balance sheet, merchant trading desk, multi-GW origination | RWE, EnBW, Eco Stor, Kyon Energy, Trianel, MW Storage | **LOW** | Requires fundamentally different capital structure (~€250–400/kWh × 100s MWh), merchant-trading capability (which aggregators like Entrix now outsource), and IPP sales motion. Viable only via JV or acquisition — not organic |

---

sections.title: Company Position Marking
sections.content:
```
L7  Grid-Connected Electrical Energy Storage Ecosystem
  L6d  Construction & Integration
    L5  Turnkey BESS EPC Integration Services          ← MEIN SOLAR PRIMARY POSITION
    L5  Civil Works & Site Preparation                 ← MEIN SOLAR SECONDARY POSITION (bundled input)
    L5  Mechanical/Electrical Installation             ← MEIN SOLAR SECONDARY POSITION (bundled input)
    L5  Split-Contract Integration Management
  L6b  Engineering & Design
    L5  Detailed System Engineering                    ← MEIN SOLAR SECONDARY POSITION (unbundle candidate)
    L5  Grid-Code Compliance Design                    ← MEIN SOLAR SECONDARY POSITION (unbundle candidate)
  L6e  Commissioning & Grid-Code Certification
    L5  SCADA / DSO Interface Commissioning            ← MEIN SOLAR SECONDARY POSITION (bundled input)
  L6f  Operations & Dispatch
    L5  O&M (BESS Operations & Maintenance)            ← MEIN SOLAR SECONDARY POSITION (PV-O&M carry-over)
```

---

sections.title: QA Checklist
sections.content:
| # | Check | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Q1 | CFJ industry-level (no solution-specific terms) | ✅ |
| Q2 | No orphan nodes (all non-L7 have parent) | ✅ |
| Q3 | Valid level transitions (no skipping) | ✅ |
| Q4 | L4 children ≥ 2 per L5 | ✅ (≥3 for Mein Solar segment; see Structured Data for full list) |
| Q5 | No solution-specific L7 or L6 names | ✅ (no "lithium", "LFP" etc. at L6+) |
| Q6 | Three-question naming test (spot-check) | ✅ |
| Q7 | Unit count 15–60 (code target) | ⚠️ ~130 — exceeds target by design per HARD RULE (full-depth to company level + all alternatives) |

---

sections.content:

rows.Value: Deliver dispatchable multi-MWh electrical energy on demand to the power system, earning revenue from flexibility, capacity, and ancillary-service markets by absorbing grid/renewable surplus and releasing it when needed
Moore 1993 — economic community of asset owners, EPC integrators, equipment OEMs, DSOs/TSOs, regulators, aggregators, O&M providers, financiers, recyclers coordinated around grid-storage outcomes

rows.Name: Site Development & Permitting

rows.Name: Engineering & Design

rows.Rationale: Low-margin commodity; only valuable as bundled EPC input, not standalone (~90% overlap)

rows.Must Add: Already owns most of L6d via single-source EPC — arguably already there
rows.Competitors at Target: Fluence, Tesla turnkey, Sungrow turnkey, Wärtsilä
rows.Economic Rationale: Mein Solar is definitionally the L6d integrator for its own projects; "moving up" means becoming the **L6d integrator for others' projects** (tier-1 EPC-for-EPC). TAM at L6d ≈ €5–8B DE BESS EPC market 2026–2030. Plausible organic path via scale-up, references, balance sheet

rows.Target (one up): **L7 Grid-Connected Electrical Energy Storage Ecosystem** (full asset owner / IPP)
rows.Must Add: Project finance, balance sheet, merchant trading desk, multi-GW origination
rows.Competitors at Target: RWE, EnBW, Eco Stor, Kyon Energy, Trianel, MW Storage
rows.Economic Rationale: Requires fundamentally different capital structure (~€250–400/kWh × 100s MWh), merchant-trading capability (which aggregators like Entrix now outsource), and IPP sales motion. Viable only via JV or acquisition — not organic
